annotations.label: Basis (informal)

anchor.snippet: a set B of elements of a vector space V is called a basis

annotations.note: Module.Basis ι R M is Mathlib's ι-indexed basis of a module M, packaged as a linear equivalence M ≃ₗ[R] (ι →₀ R).

annotations.label: Basis as linearly independent spanning set

anchor.snippet: a basis is a linearly independent spanning set

annotations.note: Basis.mk constructs a Basis from a family v satisfying LinearIndependent v and span R (range v) = ⊤.

annotations.label: All bases have the same cardinality (dimension)

anchor.snippet: all the bases have the same number of elements, called the dimension

annotations.note: mk_eq_mk_of_basis states that any two bases of the same module over a strong-rank-condition ring have equal cardinality.

annotations.label: Basis of a vector space over a field

anchor.snippet: A basis B of a vector space V over a field F

annotations.note: Module.Basis is defined over any semiring, specialising to bases of vector spaces when R is a field.

annotations.label: Linear independence condition

anchor.snippet: linear independence : for every finite subset

annotations.note: LinearIndependent R v captures the finite-sum condition via injectivity of the linear combination map.

annotations.label: Spanning property

anchor.snippet: the spanning property: for every vector

annotations.note: The spanning condition is the standard Mathlib statement Submodule.span R S = ⊤ using Submodule.span.

annotations.label: Equivalent phrasing of linear independence

anchor.snippet: The first property may be equivalently phrased as follows

annotations.note: linearIndependent_iff_notMem_span is one of several equivalent characterisations of LinearIndependent.

annotations.label: Unique representation in a basis

anchor.snippet: it follows from linear independence that this can be done in exactly one way

annotations.note: Basis.repr is itself the linear equivalence M ≃ₗ ι →₀ R, and linearCombination_repr shows the coordinates recompose x uniquely.

annotations.label: Finite-dimensional vector space

anchor.snippet: A vector space that has a finite basis is called finite-dimensional

annotations.note: FiniteDimensional K V (abbrev for Module.Finite) is Mathlib's finite-dimensionality predicate.

annotations.label: Ordered basis

anchor.snippet: one speaks of an ordered basis

annotations.note: Mathlib's Basis is inherently indexed by a type ι, so it corresponds directly to an ordered basis when ι is Fin n or another ordered index.

annotations.label: Standard basis of R^2

anchor.snippet: A simple basis of this vector space consists of the two vectors

annotations.note: Pi.basisFun R (Fin 2) is the standard basis of Fin 2 → R, i.e. the (1,0),(0,1) basis of R².

annotations.label: Standard basis of F^n

anchor.snippet: which is called the standard basis of

annotations.note: Pi.basisFun R η gives the standard basis on η → R with basis vectors Pi.single i 1.

annotations.label: Monomial basis of F[X]

anchor.snippet: One basis for this space is the monomial basis

annotations.note: MvPolynomial.basisMonomials is the monomial basis for multivariate polynomials; the univariate case (X^i basis of R[X]) is not called out under its own name.

annotations.label: Polynomial sequence basis

anchor.snippet: Any set of polynomials such that there is exactly one polynomial of each degree

annotations.note: Polynomial.Sequence.basis turns any polynomial sequence with one polynomial of each degree into a basis of R[X].

annotations.label: Steinitz exchange lemma

anchor.snippet: Many properties of finite bases result from the Steinitz exchange lemma

annotations.note: The Steinitz exchange lemma is not stated under that name; its usual consequences (extending an independent set to a basis, invariance of dimension) are present via Basis.extend and mk_eq_mk_of_basis.

annotations.label: Extending an independent set to a basis

anchor.snippet: If L is a linearly independent subset of a spanning set

annotations.note: Basis.extend extends any LinearIndepOn set to a basis of V.

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis

anchor.snippet: V has a basis (this is the preceding property

annotations.note: Basis.ofVectorSpace produces a basis of any vector space V over a division ring K.

annotations.label: Dimension theorem

anchor.snippet: All bases of V have the same cardinality

annotations.note: mk_eq_mk_of_basis is Mathlib's dimension theorem: any two bases have equal cardinality.

annotations.label: Basis as minimal generating set

anchor.snippet: A generating set S is a basis of V if and only if it is minimal

annotations.note: No direct 'basis iff minimal spanning set' characterisation found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basis as maximal independent set

anchor.snippet: A linearly independent set L is a basis if and only if it is maximal

annotations.note: Basis.maximal shows any basis is a maximal linearly independent family (over a nontrivial semiring).

annotations.label: n-element independent subset is a basis

anchor.snippet: A subset of V with n elements is a basis if and only if it is linearly independent

annotations.note: basisOfLinearIndependentOfCardEqFinrank turns a linearly independent family of size finrank K V into a basis.

annotations.label: n-element spanning subset is a basis

anchor.snippet: A subset of V with n elements is a basis if and only if it is a spanning set

annotations.note: basisOfTopLeSpanOfCardEqFinrank turns a spanning family of size finrank K V into a basis.

annotations.label: Coordinates over a basis

anchor.snippet: which are called the coordinates of v over B

annotations.note: Basis.coord i v gives the i-th coordinate of v over the basis; the whole coordinate vector is Basis.repr.

annotations.label: Coordinate isomorphism

anchor.snippet: is a linear isomorphism from the vector space

annotations.note: Basis.equivFun b : M ≃ₗ[R] ι → R is the coordinate isomorphism for finite ι (with Basis.repr for the general Finsupp version).

annotations.label: Standard/canonical basis of F^n

anchor.snippet: which is called its standard basis or canonical basis

annotations.note: Pi.basisFun R η is Mathlib's standard/canonical basis on η → R.

annotations.label: Ordered bases correspond to linear isomorphisms

anchor.snippet: it is equivalent to define an ordered basis of V , or a linear isomorphism

annotations.note: By definition a Basis is a linear equiv M ≃ₗ[R] (ι →₀ R); Basis.equivFun/Basis.ofEquivFun witnesses the correspondence with linear isos to R^n.

annotations.label: Change-of-basis formula

anchor.snippet: the change-of-basis formula is

annotations.note: Basis.toMatrix records the coordinates of one basis in another and is used to state the change-of-basis formula.

annotations.label: Matrix form of change of basis

anchor.snippet: This formula may be concisely written in matrix notation

annotations.note: Basis.toMatrix_mul_toMatrix expresses the composition law of change-of-basis matrices.

annotations.label: Module

anchor.snippet: If one replaces the field occurring in the definition of a vector space by a ring

annotations.note: Module R M is Mathlib's typeclass for a module over a semiring/ring R.

annotations.label: Free module

anchor.snippet: A module that has a basis is called a free module

annotations.note: Module.Free R M asserts M has a basis; Module.Free.exists_basis produces one.

annotations.label: Not every module has a basis

anchor.snippet: not every module has a basis

annotations.note: No named theorem asserting the negative existence 'not every module is free'; standard counterexamples like ZMod n over ℤ aren't packaged as such.

annotations.label: Subgroup of a free abelian group is free

anchor.snippet: every subgroup of a free abelian group is a free abelian group

annotations.note: Submodule.basisOfPid gives a basis for a finitely generated submodule of a free module over a PID; specialised to R=ℤ this yields freeness of finitely generated subgroups of free abelian groups, but the fully general (arbitrary rank) statement isn't stated directly under that name.

annotations.label: Hamel basis

anchor.snippet: the term Hamel basis

annotations.note: Mathlib's Module.Basis is a Hamel (algebraic) basis by default; the term 'Hamel' is not used in the library name but the concept coincides.

annotations.label: Cardinality of Hamel basis of R over Q

anchor.snippet: Hamel bases are uncountable, and have specifically the cardinality of the continuum

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement identifying the cardinality of a ℚ-basis of ℝ with the continuum.

annotations.label: Hamel basis of a Banach space is uncountable

anchor.snippet: then any Hamel basis of X is necessarily uncountable

annotations.note: The Baire-category argument giving uncountability of Hamel bases of infinite-dimensional Banach spaces is not present under any name I could find.

annotations.label: Countable Hamel basis of finitely-supported sequences

anchor.snippet: Its standard basis , consisting of the sequences having only one non-zero element

annotations.note: The space of finitely supported sequences is (ι →₀ R), and Finsupp.basisSingleOne is its standard basis of single-support vectors.

annotations.label: Orthogonal Fourier basis of square-integrable functions

anchor.snippet: are an "orthogonal basis" of the (real or complex) vector space

annotations.note: fourierBasis is Mathlib's HilbertBasis of Lp ℂ 2 haarAddCircle built from the Fourier characters.

annotations.label: Fourier basis is not a Hamel basis

anchor.snippet: cannot be represented as finite linear combinations of these basis functions

annotations.note: The remark that the Fourier basis is not a Hamel basis is an informal aside without a corresponding Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Affine basis

anchor.snippet: An affine basis for an n -dimensional affine space

annotations.note: AffineBasis ι k P is Mathlib's structure for an affine basis of an affine space.

annotations.label: Projective basis

anchor.snippet: A projective basis is

annotations.note: No 'projective basis' (frame of n+2 points in general position) definition found in Mathlib's Projectivization files.

annotations.label: Convex basis

anchor.snippet: A convex basis of a polytope is the set of the vertices

annotations.note: Mathlib has convex hulls and extreme points but does not name a 'convex basis of a polytope' as such.

annotations.label: Cone basis

anchor.snippet: A cone basis

annotations.note: No definition of a 'cone basis' (minimal set of generators of a polyhedral cone) present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Random vectors form a basis with probability one

anchor.snippet: n randomly and independently chosen vectors will form a basis with probability one

annotations.note: The probabilistic statement about n random vectors is not formalised in Mathlib.

annotations.label: ε-orthogonality

anchor.snippet: the notion of ε-orthogonality is used

annotations.note: The quantitative ε-orthogonality condition |⟨x,y⟩| ≤ ε‖x‖‖y‖ is not a named notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exponential growth of almost-orthogonal random vectors

anchor.snippet: the number of independent random vectors, which all are with given high probability pairwise almost orthogonal, grows exponentially with dimension

annotations.note: Concentration-of-measure style result about pairwise ε-orthogonal random vectors is not present.

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis (proof via Zorn)

anchor.section: Proof that every vector space has a basis
anchor.snippet: It is thus a basis of V , and this proves that every vector space has a basis

annotations.note: Basis.ofVectorSpace is Mathlib's Zorn-based existence-of-basis construction for any vector space over a division ring.

annotations.label: Existence of bases equivalent to axiom of choice

anchor.section: Proof that every vector space has a basis
anchor.snippet: if every vector space has a basis, then the axiom of choice is true

annotations.note: The Blass converse ('every vector space has a basis' ⇒ AC) is not formalised in Mathlib.
